name: Applications in Biological Engineering
description: Overview of the research and applications of biological engineering, such as bioprocessing, biotechnology, transport processes, biosensors, bioremediation, biological materials and biomedicine.
prerequisites: Prereq: BSC 2010 or equivalent.

name: Land and Water Resources Engineering
description: Introduces hydrology, flow through porous media, flood routing, grade control structures and erosion control.
prerequisites: Prereq: ENV 3040C or CGN 3421 or (COP 2271 and COP 2271L) and MAP 2302.

name: Physical and Rheological Properties of Biological Materials
description: Theory and use of physical and rheological properties of biological materials in agricultural engineering applications.
prerequisites: Prereq: CHM 2045 and MAC 2313 and PHY 2048.

name: Fundamentals and Applications of Biosensors
description: Provides a broad introduction to the field of biosensors, as well as an in-depth and quantitative view of biosensor design and performance analysis. Fundamental application of biosensor theory will be demonstrated, including: recognition, transduction, signal acquisition, and post processing/data analysis.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 and BSC 2010 and CHM 2200.

name: Remote Sensing in Engineering: Science, Sensors and Applications
description: Develop an understanding of remote sensing theory, systems and applications using information obtained from the visible/near infrared, thermal infrared and microwave regions of the EM spectrum.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 or the equivalent.

name: Controlled Environment Agriculture Systems Design
description: Introduces the engineering design of controlled-environment agriculture systems, including glazing materials selection, fan-sizing for mechanical ventilation, lighting distribution, cooling system design with fan-and-pad evaporative cooling, and heating system design with hot water floor heating.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 1147 and 3 credits of physics.

name: Life Cycle Assessment in Water-Energy-Food Systems
description: Introduction and application of life cycle assessment (LCA) to evaluate the environmental impacts of various products, processes, or services related to the water-energy-food nexus. Additional topics include planetary boundaries, systems thinking, circular economy, mass and energy balances, life-cycle costing, social LCA and sensitivity and uncertainty analysis. This is an interdisciplinary course open to students from any major.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC1147 with a minimum grade C and Junior Standing or higher.

name: Special Topics
description: Variable subjects provide content for the study of agricultural engineering topics not offered in other courses.
prerequisites: Prereq: instructor permission.

name: Special Topics
description: Variable subjects provide content for the study of agricultural engineering topics not offered in other courses.
prerequisites: Prereq: instructor permission.

name: Special Topics
description: Variable subjects provide content for the study of agricultural engineering topics not offered in other courses.
prerequisites: Prereq: instructor permission.

name: Chocolate Science and Engineering for a More Just Society
description: Unaware, we enjoy food that is produced by individuals who live in poverty.  How can innovations in biology and engineering contribute to more equitable modes of food production? How can we contribute to the effective deployment of practical technologies to improve the livelihood of people we may never interact with?

name: Introduction to Biomedical Engineering
description: Introduction to and overview of biomedical engineering. Lectures are given by faculty expert in an area of biomedical engineering. The goal is to give beginning students an appreciation for the breadth of the field and to guide them in making curriculum, major and career choices.

name: Engineering Statics and Dynamics in Biological Systems
description: Principles of engineering statics and dynamics as they relate to biological systems. Topics include vector mathematics, summation of forces and moments in static equilibrium, equations of motion, dynamics of particles and rigid bodies, concepts of work, energy, and momentum, & introduction to deformable bodies. All topics are discussed in the context of biological systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: PHY 2049 and MAC 2313.

name: Introduction to Biomolecular Engineering
description: Introduces bio-related careers in chemical engineering, providing foundational knowledge in molecular cell biology and biochemistry for early undergraduate students. Highlights the connections between biology and chemical engineering, helping students understand interdisciplinary concepts that are essential for biomolecular engineering
prerequisites:
Prereq: (Sophomore Standing or Chemical Engineering Major) and (CHM 2045 or
CHM 2095 or CHM 2050). 
Coreq:  CHM 2046 or CHM 2096 or CHM 2051.

name: Clinically-Inspired Engineering Design
description: Through exposure to real clinical problems, learn to communicate with medical professionals in order to identify unmet needs, to develop prototypes and initial concepts for clinical problems, and to critically evaluate potential solutions for clinical problems.
prerequisites: Prereq: BME 3060 and PCB 3713C with minimum grades of C; Coreq: BME 3101 and EGM 2511.

name: Computer Applications for BME
description: Computer programming lab and lecture utilizes Matlab to analyze biomedical measurements.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 2271 and COP 2271L or equivalent and MAC 2312, with minimum grades of C.

name: Biomedical Fundamentals
description: Working specifically within the framework of biomedical engineering applications, provides the engineering fundamentals of the conservation laws of mass, energy, charge, and momentum.
prerequisites: Prereq: (CHM 2046 or CHM 2096) and MAC 2313 with minimum grades of C. Coreq: PHY 2049, MAP 2302, and BME 1008.

name: Biomedical Materials
description: Restoration of physiological function by engineering biomaterials for biological environment, covering principles underlying use and design of medical implants and matrices/scaffolds. Strong emphasis on transition from engineering material to biological tissue, including molecular and cellular interactions with biomaterials, tissue and organ regeneration, and design of intact, biodegradable, and bioreplaceable materials.
prerequisites:
Prereq: BME 3060 with minimum grade of C and (CHM 3217 or (CHM2210 and
CHM2211) with minimum grades of C).

name: Cellular Engineering Laboratory
description: The cellular engineering laboratory teaches the fundamentals of cell culture for use in biomedical engineering investigations. Acquire skills in cell culture, quantitative analyses, notebook keeping, report writing, and oral presentation.
prerequisites: Prereq: PCB 3713C; Coreq: BME 4311 or instructor permission.

name: Biosignals and Systems
description: Basic theory and techniques of biosignals and systems. Topics include sampling, noise in biological signals, signal averaging of noisy biological signals, Fourier analysis and filtering.
prerequisites: Prereq: (EEL 3003 or EEL 3111C) and MAC 2313 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Molecular Biomedical Engineering
description: Introduces the fundamentals of molecular biology for biomedical engineers. Designed for juniors or seniors majoring in biomedical engineering to learn the nomenclature and current state of knowledge of the eukaryotic cell and its related structures. Topics include protein structure and function, enzymes, the structure and nature of DNA and the cellular structure and function of various cellular organelles. Learn about energy and the function of mitochondria and chloroplast, cellular communication and the function of the extracellular matrix.
prerequisites: Prereq: BSC 2010 and (CHM 3217 or CHM 2210) and PCB 3713C with minimum grades of C.

name: Neural Engineering
description: Applying engineering to neuroscience; includes such diverse areas as neural tissue engineering, models of neural function, and neural interface technology. Focuses mainly in the context of neural interfaces and prosthetics, from basic neural physiology and models of neural mechanisms to advanced neural interfaces currently in development or produced commercially.
prerequisites: Prereq: BME 3508 or EEL 3135.

name: Quantitative Physiology
description: A junior/senior level physiology course. Quantitative modeling of organ system physiology of the nervous system, the cardiovascular system and the respiratory system are discussed and students work on quantitative problems.
prerequisites: Prereq: PCB 3713C, BME 3053C, BME 3060, and BME 3508 with minimum grades of C.

name: Biomedical Instrumentation
description: Provides engineering and clinical fundamentals involved in acquiring, measuring, and processing physiological signals. Covers operational amplifier (op-amp) circuits for signal conditioning, amplification, and spectral filtering, and study biosensors and bioelectrodes used in biomedical applications such as photoplethysmography (PPG), electrocardiography (EKG), electrophoresis, and TENS. Introduces digital systems and Boolean logic, to enable prototyping clinical devices.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 and MAP 2302 and PHY 2049 and (EEL 3003 or EEL 3111C) with minimum grades of C. Coreq: BME 4503L.

name: Biomedical Instrumentation Laboratory
description: Laboratory for BME 4503.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 and MAP 2302 and PHY 2049 and (EEL 3003 or EEL 3111C) with minimum grades of C; Coreq: BME 3508 and BME 4503.

name: Medical Imaging
topics: image artifacts and quality control in mr imaging
description: Medical imaging technologies from a biomedical engineering perspective. The physics, mathematics, instrumentation and clinical applications of all common medical imaging modalities, including x-ray radiography, computed tomography (CT), ultrasound imaging, positron emission tomography (PET) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) are discussed. Emerging imaging modalities, including optical imaging, fluorescence imaging and photoacoustic imaging are also introduced.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313, MAP 2302, PHY 2049, BME 3053C, and BME 3508 with minimum grades of C.

name: Biomolecular Thermodynamics and Kinetics
description: Principles of thermodynamics and kinetics from a biomolecular perspective. The mathematics, analysis, and applications of classical thermodynamics, statistical thermodynamics, and reaction kinetics are introduced in the context of molecular interactions, binding equilibria, metabolism, and biomolecular transport common to living systems.
prerequisites:
Prereq: CHM 3217 or (CHM 2210 and CHM 2211), with minimum grades of C, and
BME 3060 and (BME 4311 or BCH 4024).

name: Biomedical Transport Phenomena
description: Introduces and applies the concepts of momentum, mass, and thermal energy transport in the context of problems of interest in biomedical sciences and engineering. Macroscopic and microscopic analysis of momentum, mass, and thermal energy transport problems in biomedical systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: BME 3060 with minimum grade of C.

name: Senior Design, Professionalism and Ethics 2
description: Design of custom strategies to address real-life issues in the development of biocompatible and biomimetic devices for biotechnology or biomedical applications. Teams work with a client in the development of projects that incorporate various aspects of biomedical engineering including instrumentation, biomechanics, biotransport, tissue engineering and others. Emphasizes formal engineering design principles; overview of intellectual properties, engineering ethics, risk analysis, safety in design and FDA regulations are reviewed. Part 2 focuses on implementation and testing.
prerequisites: Prereq: BME 4882 and (ENC 3246 with minimum grade of C) and senior standing.

name: Special Topics in Biomedical Engineering: LA Program
topics: recent developments and applications in biosensors
intro to biochemistry and molecular biology
description: Selected topics in biomedical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Biomedical Engineering: Patient Dosimetry in Med Imag
topics: recent developments and applications in biosensors
intro to biochemistry and molecular biology
description: Selected topics in biomedical engineering.

name: Theory and Practice of Multimedia Production
description: Combines the traditional media production pipeline and software engineering processes to synthesize an approach geared for the production of works incorporating both artistic and computational elements.
prerequisites: Prereq: CAP 3027.

name: Interactive Modeling and Animation 1
description: Introduces programming and data structures for interactive two-dimensional multimedia applications. Representing form and transforms in two dimensions, capturing user actions and driving application behavior interactively. Graphical interfaces, image processing, automata and basic artificial intelligence.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 1147 or equivalent.

name: Introduction to Computer-Aided Animation
description: Introduces topics related to computer-aided animation. Rigging for forward and inverse kinematics. Skin weighting. Morph targets. Expression-driven animation, rigid-body and particle simulation.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 1147 or equivalent.

name: Natural Language Processing
description: Introduction to the essential concepts, principles, and techniques of Natural Language Processing (NLP). Practical application and theoretical concepts are examined. Topics include information extraction, language construction, grammars, disambiguation, as well as system modeling, classification, and evaluation.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Computational Structures in Computer Graphics
description: Studies the major topics in computer graphics: display and output technology, two and three dimensional manipulations; space curves and surfaces, hidden surface removal and shading models.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Introduction to Computer Organization
description: Organization of computing systems. Logical basis of computer structure. Machine representation of instructions and data, flow of control, and basic machine instructions. Assembly language programming.
prerequisites: Prereq: (COP 3504 or COP 3503) and (MAC 2233 or MAC 2311 or MAC 3472) and COT 3100.

name: Embedded Systems
description: Design of efficient and trustworthy embedded and cyber-physical systems consisting of hardware, software, firmware, sensors, and actuators. Covers fundamental issues related to modeling and specification, design space exploration, hardware-software partitioning, synthesis and compilation, real-time operating systems, and application-specific optimizations targeting area, power, performance, temperature, energy, and security.
prerequisites: Prereq: CDA 3101 with minimum grade of C.

name: Soil Mechanics
description: Physical properties of soils, compaction, flow of water through soil, distribution of stress within soil and consolidation. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520.

name: Geotechnical Engineering
description: Subsurface exploration settlements analysis, slope stability, earth pressure and an introduction to foundation design.
prerequisites: Prereq: CEG 4011.

name: Introduction to Software Engineering
description: Topics include software planning, specifications, coding, testing and maintenance. Gain experience in the team approach to large system development.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Human-Computer Interaction
description: Studies the major topics in the study, planning and design of the interaction between people and computers. Topics include interface design (principles, theories and guidelines), virtual environments, interactive devices and collaboration.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: User Experience Design
description: Introduces methods and tools used in User Experience Design (UXD): the early stages of software design focused on meeting user needs. Key concepts include user research, contextual design, design thinking, ideation, iterative design, prototyping, and design documentation. Projects utilize software tools used in the industry.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Mechanics of Engineering Structures
description: Introduces structural load, equilibrium, shear and bending moment diagrams, structural analysis software, classical methods for displacement determination, method of consistent deformations, slope deflection method, moment distribution method.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520.

name: Analysis and Design in Reinforced Concrete
topics: analysis and design of reinforced concrete
description: Ultimate strength analysis and design of reinforced beams and columns, working stress design for flexure, design of footings and retaining walls.
prerequisites: Prereq: CES 3102 and CGN 3501C and Engineering major.

name: Introduction to Civil Engineering
description: Introduces the broad field of civil engineering.

name: Computer Methods in Civil Engineering
description: Review of computer programming. Numerical methods as applied to civil engineering problems and civil engineering software.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 2271 or COP 2273.

name: Civil Engineering Materials
description: Studies the principal materials used for engineering purposes with special attention to mechanical properties and their importance to the engineer. Hands-on experience in testing of civil engineering materials.
prerequisites: Coreq: EGM 3520.

name: Civil Engineering Practice
description: Fundamentals of civil engineering professional practice: project management, construction delivery processes, business concepts, public policy, administration, diversity and equity issues in civil engineering, and leadership.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 2511 or equivalent. Coreq: CGN 2328.

name: Applied Data Science in Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: Introduces the workflows of data science applications, covering the state-of-art techniques in data acquisition, data processing and management, analytics and modeling, and visualization. Critical application of data science concepts and techniques in complex socioeconomic and environmental contexts. Basics of problem formulation and major ethical considerations of applying data science in practice.
prerequisites: Prereq: CGN 3421 or ENV 3040C or equivalent.

name: Public Works Engineering and Management Practices
description: Public works profession, organization, administration, and management of operating divisions with emphasis on role of engineer.
prerequisites: Prereq: CGN 4160.

name: Transportation-Water-Materials Design
description: Simulation of a design project experience through the completion and presentation of a comprehensive roadway project design. Students work in multi-disciplinary groups to complete a system design that includes traffic, materials, hydrologic and geotechnical considerations.
prerequisites: Prereq: senior standing.

name: Structures-Geotechnical-Construction Comprehensive System Design
description: Simulation of a design office experience through the completion and presentation of a comprehensive building design. Students work in multi-disciplinary groups to complete a system design that includes structural, geotechnical and construction management considerations.
prerequisites: Prereq: instructor permission.

name: Penetration Testing: Ethical Hacking
description: Introduces principles and techniques associated with the cybersecurity practice known as penetration testing or ethical hacking. Covers planning, reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, and result reporting. Discover how system vulnerabilities can be exploited and learn to avoid such problems.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Enterprise Security
description: Provides an introduction to the real-world aspects of defending an enterprise network. Students will gain hands-on experience performing system security tasks and handling incidents. The class begins with a basic introduction to enterprise cybersecurity, the attack sequence, and managing cybersecurity. Then lecture, homework and lab activities cover the center for internet security's twenty essential security controls.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Information and Database Systems 1
description: First part of a two-course sequence that studies the essential concepts, principles and techniques of modern database systems. Topics include modeling and querying of data using conceptual data models as well as the development of a database application.
prerequisites: Prereq: (COP 3504 or COP 3503) and COT 3100.

name: Computer and Information Security
description: Covers systematic threat and risk assessment; programmed threats and controls in hardware, software, and human procedures; security policies, models, and mechanisms; theoretical limitations and practical implementations; certification and accreditation standards; and case study reviews. Includes projects.
prerequisites: Prereq: CDA 3101.

name: CS Teaching & Learning
description: Covers basic pedagogy, especially as it relates to computer science and engineering, and covers three fundamental elements in education: learning environment, educational theory, and educational practice as approached to engineering-specific training.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3502 or COP 3504C.

name: Senior Project
description: Involves completing a significant CISE-related project. Coordinate with the instructor and a project advisor, prepare a detailed technical report and deliver an oral presentation.
prerequisites: Prereq: senior CISE standing or higher and approved project proposal.

name: Computer Network Fundamentals
description: Fundamental concepts, principles, and standards of computer networks. Introduces topics in top-down approach, starting with the application layer in the OSI system architecture with a stronger focus on application, transport, and network layers.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530. Coreq: COP 4600.

name: Computer Programming for Engineers: Comp Prg Engr VB.NET
description: Computer programming and the use of computers to solve engineering and mathematical problems. Emphasizes applying problem solving skills; directed toward technical careers in fields employing a reasonably high degree of mathematics. The programming language used depends on the demands of the departments in the college. Several languages may be taught each semester, no more than one per section. Those required to learn a specific language must enroll in the correct section.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2312 with minimum grade of C.

name: Programming Fundamentals 1
description: First course of a two-semester introductory sequence for those planning further study in computer science, digital arts and sciences or computer engineering. Concepts of computer science and the process of computer programming, including object-oriented programming, procedural and data abstraction and program modularity.
prerequisites: Coreq: MAC 2311.

name: Programming Fundamentals 2
description: Second course of a two-semester introductory sequence for those planning further study in computer science, digital arts and sciences or computer engineering. Concepts of computer science and the process of computer programming, including object-oriented programming, procedural and data abstraction and program modularity.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3502C and MAC 2311 both with minimum grades of C.

name: Data Structures and Algorithm
description: Algorithm development using pseudo languages, basic program structures, program design techniques, storage and manipulation of basic data structures like arrays, stacks, queues, sorting and searching and string processing. Linked linear lists. Trees and multilinked structures.
prerequisites: Prereq: (COP 3504 or COP 3503) and COT 3100 and (MAC 2234 or MAC 2312 or MAC 2512 or MAC 3473), all with a minimum grade of C.

name: Programming Language Concepts
description: Introduces programming language principles, including language constructs, design goals, run-time structures, implementation techniques and exposure to a wide variety of programming paradigms.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Object-oriented Programming
description: Fundamental conceptual models for programming languages illustrated with specific programming languages and application problems. Specific topics include class and object models, inheritance among classes, objects and static and dynamic systems and implementations.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Algorithm Abstraction and Design
description: Covers algorithmic concepts and their use rooted in practical application and computer science theory. Topics include algorithmic paradigms, limits of computing, and algorithm time complexity classes.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 3530.

name: Operating Systems
description: Design and implementation of various components of a modern operating system, including I/O programming, interrupt handling, process and resource management, computer networks and distributed systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: CDA 3101 and COP 3530; knowledge of C or C++ recommended.

name: Applications of Discrete Structures
description: Covers the mathematics of discrete events; i.e., events that involve distinct elements, finite structures of distinct elements or finite sampled versions of continuous phenomena (such as movement).
prerequisites: Prereq: (MAC 2311 or MAC 3472) and (COP 3502C or equivalent), all with a minimum grades of C; Coreq: COP 3504 or COP 3503.

name: Computer Model Formulation
description: Solutions of scientific and engineering problems using digital computers. Formulation of models for describing physical processes, numerical analysis, and computer programming.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 2273 with a grade of C or better and MAP 2302 and MAC 2313.

name: Numerical Analysis: a Computational Approach
description: Numerical integration, nonlinear equations, linear and nonlinear systems of equations, differential equations and interpolation.
prerequisites: Prereq: (COP 3504 or COP 3503) and MAS 3114.

name: Hydrodynamics
description: Classification and properties of fluids, hydrostatics, and conservation of mass, momentum and energy in fluid flow. Potential flow, similitude and physical modeling. Laminar and turbulent pipe flow. Introduces turbomachines.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 with minimum grade of C and EGM 2511.

name: Hydraulics
description: Fundamental equations for pipe and open conduit flow. Development of design oriented formulas for pipes and open channels. Introduces hydrology.
prerequisites: Prereq: CWR 3201 or instructor permission.

name: Introduction to Aerospace Engineering
description: Overview of aerospace engineering. Standard atmosphere, basic aerodynamics, airplane performance, stability and control, propulsion, and space flight.
prerequisites: Credits: 3; Prereq: PHY 2048 or PHY 2060, with minimum grade of C.

name: Aerodynamics
description: Incompressible aerodynamics, integral and differential governing equations, potential flow, boundary layers, airfoils, wings, numerical techniques.
prerequisites: Prereq: (EAS 2011 or EAS 3020C or EGN 3353C) and COP 2271 and EML 3100 and MAC 2313 and MAP 2302 with minimum grades of C.

name: Compressible Flow
topics: adiabatic flow in a duct with friction
flow with heat addition of removal
description: One-dimensional and quasi one-dimensional compressible fluid flows. Includes mach waves, normal shocks, oblique shocks, Prandtl-Meyer expansions, isentropic flow with area change, Fanno flow and Rayleigh flow.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4101 or EGN 3353C

name: Aerospace Structures
description: Review of plane states of stress and strain. Includes analysis of thin-walled beams with open and closed section, unsymmetrical bending of wing sections, torsion of skin-stringer and multi-cell sections, flexural shear in open and closed sections, Shear Center and failure criteria. Also includes introduction to composite materials and demonstration of behavior of some simple structural elements.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520 with minimum grade of C.

name: Aerospace Composites
topics: stress analysis of laminated composite plates
analysis of laminates for in-plane loading
analysis of laminates for bending moments
analysis of laminates for hygrothermal effects
strength of laminates with mechanical loading
description: Various types and applications of structural composites used in flight structures. Also includes an introduction to analysis of structural composites.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520 with minimum grade of C.

name: Aerospace Propulsion
description: Basics of air-breathing and rocket engines used in flight systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4132.

name: Stability and Control of Aircraft
description: Static stability and control, equations of motion, stability derivatives, stability of longitudinal and lateral motion of aircraft.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4101 and EML 4312.

name: Astrodynamics
topics: time dependence of r and v
description: Introduces the solar system. Includes study of two-body motion, Hohmann transfer, patched conics for interplanetary and lunar trajectories, and the restricted three-body problem. Also includes an introduction to powered flights and artificial satellite orbits.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3401 with minimum grade of C and (MAP 4305 or MAP 5304).

name: Space Systems Design
description: A discussion of the component systems of a spacecraft and a typical mission's requirements. The operation and character of different spacecraft hardware is presented as well as typical mission timelines from early conception to final operations. Topics include the space environment, guidance/control/navigation systems, spacecraft sensors and actuators, propulsion systems, thermal systems, power systems, launch systems, communication systems, structural systems and mission operations. This course is useful to engineers, scientists, computer scientists and any profession that uses data.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4510.

name: Aerospace Design 1
topics: human spaceflight mission analysis and design
description: Applications of the principles of analysis and design to aerospace vehicles. Emphasizes astronautics.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4510 and EML 4312 and EGM 3520.

name: Aerospace Design 2
description: Applications of the principles of analysis and design to aerospace vehicles. Emphasizes aeronautics.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4101 and EAS 4400 and EGM 3520.

name: Aerospace Sciences Lab and Design
description: Experimental investigations of aerospace engineering systems. Wind tunnel testing. Design project with experimental validation.
prerequisites: Prereq: EAS 4101 and EAS 4132 and EML 3301C.

name: Professional Development of Chemical Engineers
description: Introduces the Chemical Engineering profession and a wide range of careers, including chemical, energy, pharmaceutical, semiconductor, and food industries, as well as graduate and professional school pathways. Provides resources to build successful professional careers, including best practices for interviewing, oral presentations, and teamwork. Also discusses professional codes of conduct and ethical decision-making.

name: Material and Energy Balances
topics: non-reactive systems involving an energy balance
description: Formulation and solution of material and energy balances utilizing physical/chemical properties of matter as applied to analyzing unit operations systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: (CHM 2045 or CHM 2095 or CHM 2050) & MAC 2312 & PHY 2048; Coreq: (CHM 2046 or CHM 2096 or CHM 2051) & MAC 2313 & MAP 2302 & ECH 2934

name: Process Thermodynamics
description: Introduces fundamental principles of classical and statistical thermodynamics. Application of the first and second laws of thermodynamics to the modeling and analysis of physical and chemical processes undergoing change, such as liquefaction, refrigeration, and power generation.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 2273 and ECH 3023. Coreq: COT 3502.

name: Fluid and Solid Operations
description: Characteristics of laminar and turbulent flow, mechanical energy balance, flow through packed beds and fluidization of solids, design of pumping systems and piping networks and metering of fluids.
prerequisites: Prereq: COT 3502 and ECH 3264.

name: Energy Transfer Operations
topics: mathematical description of heat transfer problems
fundamentals of heat and mass transfer
fundamentals of momentum, heat, and mass transfer
description: Steady state conduction in solids and heterogeneous materials, transient conduction, convection heat transfer, heat transfer during boiling and condensation, radiation heat transfer, design of heat-transfer equipment and heat exchange networks.
prerequisites: Prereq: COT 3502 and ECH 3264.

name: Elementary Transport Phenomena
description: Flux law and conservation equations of mass, energy, and momentum; solution of transport problems associated with physical and chemical processing; macroscopic and microscopic analysis.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3023 and MAP 2302 and MAC 2313.

name: Phase and Chemical Equilibria
description: Application of thermodynamic principles to systems of variable composition, including the study of phase and chemical equilibria.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3101.

name: Fluid and Energy Transfer Operations Laboratory
topics: fluid and energy transfer operations laboratory
description: Laboratory work in unit operations involving heat and momentum transfer.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3101 and ECH 3203 and ECH 3223 and STA 3032 or STA 2023; Coreq: ECH 4714 and ENC 2256.

name: Process Control Theory
description: Analysis and automatic control of process systems in chemical engineering.
prerequisites: Prereq: COT 3502. Coreq: ECH 4323L.

name: Process Control Laboratory
topics: frequency response software that handles deadtime
description: Laboratory work associated with ECH 4323.
prerequisites:
Prereq: COT 3502.
Coreq: ECH 4323.

name: Separation and Mass Transfer Operations
description: Theory, design, and evaluation of diffusional and staged mass transfer processes including distillation, absorption and extraction, leaching, and membrane separations. Computer-aided design methods.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3101 and ECH 3203 and ECH 3223.

name: Separation and Mass Transfer Operations Laboratory
topics: separation and mass transfer operations laboratory
description: Laboratory work in unit operations involving mass transfer. (WR)
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 4403 and ECH 4224L and ECH 4714.

name: Chemical Kinetics and Reactor Design
description: Homogeneous and heterogeneous reaction kinetic modeling and data analysis. Analysis and design of ideal batch, mixed, plug and recycle reactors. Heterogeneous catalysis and reactor design.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3264 and ECH 4123.

name: Process Economics and Optimization
description: Introduces the principles of process economics including specifications and costing of equipment, operations costing and economic evaluation of processes.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3203 and ECH 3223; Coreq: ECH 4403.

name: Process Design
description: Preliminary design of convention chemical processes including process specifications, sitting and layout, equipment sizing, utility and manpower needs, safety and hazard analysis, environmental considerations and economic evaluation. Planning techniques for detailed engineering, construction and startup.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 4403 and ECH 4504 and ECH 4604 and ECH 4824.

name: Chemical Process Safety
description: Laboratory and process safety analysis which emphasizes prevention and mitigation. Application of chemical engineering principles to assessing hazards and risk.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3101, ECH 3203 and ECH 3223.

name: Materials of Chemical Engineering
description: Relations between microscopic structure and macroscopic mechanical, thermal and electrical properties of organic and inorganic solids. Engineering applications, including corrosion.
prerequisites: Prereq: ECH 3264

name: Technology and Its Impact on Society
description:
Learn about electronic technology developments over the last 100 years and
their impact on society – entertainment, politics, military, industry, etc.

name: Electronic Circuits 1
description: Fundamentals of electronic circuits and systems. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3111C with minimum grade of C.

name: Solid-State Electronic Devices
description: Introduces the principles of semiconductor electron device operation. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3008.

name: VLSI Circuits and Technology 1
description: Introduction to VLSI circuit technology and manufacturing. Fabrication, device models, layout, parasitics, and simple gate circuits.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEE 3308C and EEL 3701C.

name: Future of Microelectronics Technology
description: Surveys state-of-the-art microelectronics technology and prospects for future technologies. Topics include nanoscale MOSFETs, strained Si, high-k gate dielectrics, carbon nanotubes, molecular electronics and single-electron devices.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEE 3396 or equivalent.

name: Introduction to Quantum Computing
description: Introduces quantum computing concepts and hardware implementations in a self-contained manner and discusses fundamentals of quantum mechanics. Also introduces important concepts of superposition and entanglement, hardware realization of quantum computing technologies, and quantum computing algorithms.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEE 3396 with a minimum grade of C and (MAS 3114 with a minimum grade of C or EEL 3850 with a minimum grade of C).

name: Real Time Digital Signal Processing Applications
description: Real world digital signal processing (DSP) tasks are presented and solved in a lab environment that utilizes a Floating Point DSP and a development simulation and hardware emulation tool. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3135 and EEL 3744C.

name: Acoustics
description: Governing equations for wave theory of sound; Character of plane acoustic waves and 3-D acoustic fields; Sound transmission/reflection at an interface between two media; Waves transmission/attenuation inducts; Low frequency approximations (lumped-element modeling) and transducers; sources of sound.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 and (EEL 3111C or EEL 3003) with minimum grades of C or instructor permission.

name: Fundamentals of Machine Learning
description: Overview of machine intelligence and the role of machine learning in a variety of real-world problems. Probability and statistics to handle uncertain data. Topics covered include: learning models from data in both a supervised and unsupervised fashion, linear models and non- linear models for classification, and linear dimensionality reduction.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3135 and EEL 3850 with minimum grades of C.

name: Neural Signals, Systems, and Technology
description: Biophysical principles of neural signaling; characterization of neural circuits and systems; technology design principles for interfacing with biological neural systems; overview of clinical and consumer applications for neurotechnology and artificial intelligence.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3850 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Introduction to Electrical Engineering
description: Introduces the profession of electrical engineering. Presents career development and enhancement, professional codes of conduct, ethics, entrepreneurship, intellectual property, professional societies. Projects provide hands-on experience with soldering, MATLAB programming, and microcontrollers.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2311 with minimum grade of C or appropriate AP/IB score and Electrical Engineering majors only.

name: Physics of Electrical Engineering
description: Introduces the fundamental physics underlying components and devices and their application to electronics, power, and wireless.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3111C and MAC 2313 and MAP 2302.

name: Circuits 1
topics: response of first-order rl and rc circuits
the natural and step rlc response
description: Basic analysis of DC and AC electric circuits. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2312 and PHY 2049.

name: Circuits 2
description: Continuous-time signals and linear systems: Fourier series and transforms, frequency, response, Laplace transform and system function, analog filters; emphasis on electrical circuits. Sampling.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3000 and EEL 3111C and EEL 3135 and EGN 2020C and MAP 2302, all with minimum grades of C.

name: Introduction to Signals and Systems
description: Continuous-time and discrete-time signal analysis including Fourier series and discrete-time and discrete Fourier transforms; sampling; discrete-time linear system analysis with emphasis on FIR and IIR systems: impulse response, frequency response, and system function; MATLAB-based programming for Signals and Systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 and (EEL 3834 or COP 3503Cor COP 3504C or COP 2274) all with minimum grades of C.

name: Remote Sensing in Engineering: Science, Sensors and Applications
description: Remote sensing theory, systems and applications using information obtained from the visible/near infrared, thermal infrared and microwave regions of the EM spectrum.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 or equivalent.

name: Fundamentals of Electromagnetic Fields
description: Transmission lines, vector analysis, electrostatics, magnetostatics, time-varying fields, plane waves.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3008.

name: Digital Logic and Computer Systems
description: Overview of logic design, algorithms, computer organization and assembly language programming and computer engineering technology. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: Knowledge of a programming language.

name: Programming for Electrical Engineering 1
description: Develops computer skills and the art of writing sound computer programs using examples and exercises relevant to electrical and computer engineering.

name: Data Science for ECE
description: Analysis, processing, simulation, and reasoning of data. Includes data conditioning and plotting, linear algebra, statistical methods, probability, simulation, and experimental design.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2312 and (EEL 3834 or COP 3503 or COP 3504C or COP 2274), all with minimum grades of C.

name: Electrical Engineering Design 1
description: Teams design, produce, and report on a hardware prototype, meeting defined specifications and using a structured design methodology. Includes project management, hardware prototyping, and project reporting.
prerequisites: Prereq: (EEE 3308C and EEL 3112 and EEL 3701C with minimum grades of C) and 2 courses from breadth elective list.

name: Power System Analysis
description: Development of power system equivalents by phase network analysis, load flow, symmetrical components, sequence networks, and fault analysis.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3211C.

name: Wireless and Mobile Networks
topics: principles of wireless access and localization
description: Senior-level study of wireless and mobile networks. Investigates telecommunication architectures and protocols for wireless sensor networks and wireless embedded systems; Wi-Fi and wireless local area networks; mobile ad-hoc networks; next generation cellular systems and satellite networks.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3701C.

name: State Variable Methods in Linear Systems
description: Development of state-variable approach to linear continuous-time and discrete-time systems with emphasis on the design of feedback control systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 4657C.

name: Digital Design
topics: intel® quartus® prime pro edition user guides
description: Advanced modular logic design, design languages, finite state machines and binary logic. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3701C.

name: Digital Computer Architecture
description: The use of electronic digital modules to design computers. Includes the organization and operation of computers, hardware/software trade-offs and design of computer interfacing. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3701C and EEL 4712C.

name: Advanced Systems Programming
description: Develop a deep understanding of operating system concepts and systems programming fundamentals and gain hands-on experience in systems programming by using Pthreads as well as implementing Linux device drivers and testing/verifying systems code for deadlock and race-freedom.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3701C and (COP 2271 or COP 2273 or COP 2274 or COP 3502 or COP 3502C or COP 3503 or COP 3503C or COP 3504 or COP 3504C or EEL 3834 or equivalent) and COP 4600.

name: Microprocessor Applications
description: Experience in the elements of microprocessor-based systems, hardware interfacing and software design for their application. Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3701C (with minimum grade of C) and (EEL 3834 or COP 3503C or COP 3504C or COP 2274 or equivalent with minimum grades of C).

name: Programming for Electrical Engineering 2
description: Fundamentals of data structures and algorithms, including lists, queues, stacks, divide-and-conquer, dynamic programming, trees, tables, graphs and recursive techniques. The role of specific data structures in electrical engineering applications.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3834 or COP 2274 or COP 3503C or COP 3504C or equivalent, all with minimum grades of C.

name: Cross Layered System Security
description: Develop an understanding of the principles of computer security, as it crosses layers of abstraction (application, operating system, hardware, and network). Learn the challenges of building secure computer systems with examples and hands-on assignments. Current research on these challenges will be discussed. Students will review and present conference papers.
prerequisites:
Prereq: (EEL 3834 or COP 3503C or COP 3504C or COP 2274 or
equivalent) and (EEL 4736 or equivalent), all with a minimum grades of C.

name: Individual Problems in Electrical Engineering
description: Selected problems or projects in the student's major field of engineering study.

name: Electrical Engineering Design 2: ELEC ENGR DESIGN 2
description: Selected design projects involving engineering applications in the various areas of electrical engineering.  Laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEL 3923C and two breadth electives and one depth elective.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Power Electronics II
topics: full bridge and half bridge converters
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Autonomous Robots
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Intro Biomed Image Analysis
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: System-on-Chip Design
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Intro Quantum Dev Tech
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Intro Neural Interfaces & Sys
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Image Processing / Computer V
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Intro Semiconductor Packaging
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Nano & Quantum Photonics App
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Applied Machine Learning Sys
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: Micronano Mach Metama
description: Special courses covering selected topics in electrical engineering.

name: Engineering Mechanics: Statics
description: Reduction of force systems, equilibrium of particles and rigid bodies, vector methods and their application to structures and mechanisms.
prerequisites: Prereq: PHY 2048; Coreq: MAC 2313.

name: Introduction to Numerical Methods of Engineering Analysis
description: Methods for numerical solution of mathematical problems with emphasis on engineering applications using MATLAB. Includes roots, optimization, linear algebraic equations, matrices, curve fitting, differentiation, integration and ordinary differential equations.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 and COP 2271; Coreq: MAP 2302.

name: Engineering Mechanics: Dynamics
topics: dynamics of particles and rigid bodies
description: Kinematics and dynamics of particles and rigid bodies in one, two, and three dimensions. Work-energy and impulse-momentum concepts.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 2511 and MAC 2313.

name: Mechanics of Materials
description: Stress and strain at a point, stress-strain-temperature relations and mechanical properties of materials. Systems subject to axial load, torsion and bending. Design concepts, indeterminate structures and applications.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 2511 (not EGM 2500) and MAC 2313.

name: Modeling and Control of Biomolecular Machines
description: Overview of biomolecular systems engineering. Introduction to cell processes, biochemical kinetics, models of biological macromolecules, analyses of biomolecular dynamics, simulation of stochastic behaviors, common gene regulatory network motifs, and the design of synthetic biology circuits.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Biodynamics
description: Dynamic analysis of the human musculoskeletal system. Includes development of lumped mass, planar rigid body and 3-D rigid body models of human movement. Also includes calculation of internal forces in muscles and joints and analysis of muscle function using dynamics principles and musculoskeletal geometry.
prerequisites: Credits: 3; Prereq: EGM 3400 or EGM 3401, or instructor permission.

name: Fluid Mechanics
description: Statics and dynamics of incompressible fluids. Application to viscous and inviscid flows. Dimensional analysis. Compressible flow.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 with a minimum grade of C and EGM 2511 and (EML 3100 or EML 3007 or BME 3060).

name: Introduction to Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: Introduction to the field of industrial and systems engineering. Provides an overview of methodological and application areas as well as career paths.

name: Human Factors & Ergonomics
description: Introduces the techniques/concepts to understand users and workplace requirements for the design of sociotechnical systems. Topics covered include methods for work measurement, human cognitive and physical capabilities and limitations, and workplace requirements. Applications for design, including computer displays, noise, repetitive and high physical effort tasks are presented.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3215C with minimum grade of C.

name: Engineering Economy
description: Basic principles and applications of economic decision-making between alternatives encountered in engineering systems projects. Analysis includes methodologies of economics and finance in addition to engineering fundamentals.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2312 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Occupational Safety Engineering
description:
Safety history and litigation; accident causation; safety organizations and
agencies. Approaches to occupational safety and risk management. Product defects and safety program development; product liability; consumer product safety commission. Hazard communication standard. Workers’ compensation. OSHA safety standards and codes; OSHA record keeping. Common occupational hazards.
prerequisites: Prereq: PHY 2049 and ESI 3215C with minimum grades of C.

name: Human Factors Applications
description: Focuses on applications of advanced topics in human factors and design within various industrial engineering related domains. Students will be introduced to important domains for human factors work in industry and academia, such as user experience in information technology, healthcare human factors, traffic safety and driving, aviation, and command and control. Students will apply human factors methods and concepts to problems within these domains through case study projects and assignments.
prerequisites: Prereq: EIN 3241 with a minimum grade of C and ESI 3215C with a minimum grade of C.

name: Senior Design Project
description: Integration of industrial and systems engineering methodologies; emphasizes methods of successful implementation. Project and case-study oriented.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3312, ESI 4313, ESI 4523, ESI 4356, EIN 3354, and EGS 4034 all with minimum grades of C.

name: Inventory and Supply Chain Systems
description: Develops analytic abilities to formulate and solve inventory and logistics problems faced by today's firms. Learn to take a comprehensive view of complex inventory and supply-chain systems; develop abilities to model, optimize and design such systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3312.

name: Facility Planning and Material Handling
description: Introduces fundamental concepts in several main areas of industrial engineering such as facility planning, material handling systems, unit loading and warehouse picking. Topics such as analysis and design of workspace and flow, facility location and layout, material handling systems, and optimization of picking processes are covered.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3312 (C) and (EML 2023 or equivalent) with minimum grades of C.

name: Lean Production Systems
description: Design of flow line, cellular and flexible manufacturing systems. Design and control of lean manufacturing systems. Continuous improvement, small lot production, setup-time reduction, equipment improvement and maintenance. Principles and control of push and pull manufacturing systems. Production planning and operations scheduling.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3312 (C) and ESI 3215C (C).

name: Sophomore Materials Laboratory
description: Conceptual perspective of the origin of materials behavior and the interrelationships of the materials tetrahedron: structure/property/performance/processing. Conduct experiments on the materials tetrahedron.
prerequisites: Coreq: EMA 3010.

name: Materials
description: Conceptual perspective for origin of materials behavior and the interrelationships of structure/property/performance. Materials selection and use of familiar material (metals, ceramics, polymers, electronic materials and composites) in electronics and structural and other engineering applications.
prerequisites: Prereq: CHM 2045 or CHM 2095.

name: Fundamental Principles of Materials
topics: materials design via elasticity and plasticity
description: Covers the fundamental principles of structure, reactivity and energies describing materials systems, directly relating individual principles to specific materials properties or functions.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3010.

name: Materials Laboratory 2
description: General undergraduate materials laboratory. (WR)
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3080C.

name: Electronic Properties of Materials
description: Atomistic and quantum-mechanical description of the electrical, optical, magnetic and thermal properties of materials. Deals with metals, alloys, semiconductors, polymers, dielectrics and amorphous materials with special emphasis given to high technology applications of electronic materials.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3011

name: Analysis of the Structure of Materials
description: Laboratory fundamentals of crystallography, x-ray and electron diffraction, scanning and transmission electron microscopy, surface analysis and microprobe techniques.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3010.

name: Error Analyses and Optimization Methodologies in Materials Research
description: Statistical approach for materials research, basic and relevant statistical concepts, error analyses, factorial matrices, reducing the variance, nested designs and sampling plans, mixture designs, optimization technology, response surface method and Taguchi.
prerequisites: Coreq: EMA 3010 and (COP 2271 or COP 2273).

name: Metallurgy Laboratory
description: Concepts, skills, and techniques required for an understanding of metals and metallurgy processing.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3050; Coreq: EMA 4120.

name: Biomaterials Laboratory
description: Hands-on laboratory experience in the processing and characterization of biomaterials for use in medical applications.
prerequisites: Coreq: EMA 4061.

name: Biopolymers: Manufacture, Stability and Biocompatibility
description: Polymer manufacturing processes and biochemical/biophysical behavior are considered from the perspective of achieving those properties needed for the engineering of polymeric implants and devices. Unique economic, ethical and regulatory issues are also presented.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3066.

name: Kinetics of Materials
description: Science and application of diffusion and phase transformations in alloys, semiconductors, ceramics, and polymers, phenomenological description and atomic theory, analytical and numerical solutions, solidification and solid-state transformations, including nucleation, growth, and coarsening.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 4314.

name: Mechanical Behavior of Materials
description: Plastic deformation and fracture of metals and alloys, ceramics and polymers.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520.

name: Electronic Materials Laboratory
description: Hands-on experience for those specializing in electronic materials. Laboratory topics include characterization of optical and electronic properties of semiconductor materials, electronic devices characterization and semiconductor processing.
prerequisites: Coreq: EMA 4614.

name: Li-ion Next Generation Batteries
topics: anode materials for lithium ion batteries
description: Qualities and reactions that characterize Li-ion batteries and other electrochemical cells. Emphasis on mechanical properties. Li-ion and Sodium-ion batteries, fuel cells, capacitors, and hydrogen storage.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3520 with a minimum grade of C and EMA 3010.

name: Compound Semiconductor Materials
topics: principles of electronic materials and devices
description: Physical properties of technologically important compound semiconductor materials. Epitaxial growth and practical application of compound semiconductor heterostructures.
prerequisites: Prereq: EEE 3396.

name: Process Metallurgy
description: Engineering aspects of mineral processing, including unit operations and flow sheets. Science and technology of metal extraction with applications to specific ferrous and non-ferrous metals.
prerequisites: Coreq: EMA 4120.

name: Polymer Processing
description: Major processing methods for polymers and polymeric composites as related to the rheological behavior of these systems. Synthesis of polymers via industrial processes.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3066.

name: Materials Selection and Failure Analysis
description: Philosophy and practice of engineering selection of materials. Case studies in product liability and failure analysis.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 4223 and EMA 4324.

name: Research in Materials Science and Engineering 2
description: Continuation of EMA 4913: short research problems in materials science and engineering, usually including a final thesis.

name: Special Topics: AI for Materials
description: Laboratory, lectures or conferences covering selected topics in materials science and engineering.

name: Computer Aided Graphics and Design
description: Sketching, descriptive geometry, computer graphics, computer aided drafting and design projects.

name: Design and Manufacturing Laboratory
description: Study and application of design, problem formulation, conceptual design, prototype development. Study of common manufacturing processes.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML2023 & (Aerospace Engineering or Mechanical Engineering major).

name: Mechanical Engineering Design 1
description: Design process, kinematics, gear trains, and standard mechanical components.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP 2271 and EML 2322L and EGM 3520 with a minimum grade of C and EGM 3401 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Thermodynamics
topics: first and second laws of thermodynamics
fossil fuel powered fired power plants
entropy and 2nd law for isolated systems
description: Application of the first and second laws of thermodynamics to closed and open systems and to cyclic heat engines. Includes the development of procedures for calculating the properties of multiphase and single-phase pure substances.
prerequisites: Credits: 3; Prereq: CHM 2045, MAC 2313 and PHY 2048.

name: Mechanics of Materials Laboratory
description: Experimental characterization of the mechanical properties of engineering materials, precision instruments, computer-based data acquisition, statistical uncertainty analysis, preparation of engineering reports.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3010 and (EGM 3520 with a minimum grade of C) and COP 2271 and (ENC 2210 or ENC 3254 or ENC 3246).

name: Heat Transfer
description: Steady state and transient analysis of conduction and radiation heat transfer in stationary media. Also discusses heat transfer in fluid systems, including forced and free convection.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAP 2302 with minimum grade of C and (EAS 4101 or EGN 3353C).

name: Thermal Sciences Design and Laboratory
description: Thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and heat transfer integrated with design and laboratory.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 3100 with a minimum grade of C and EML 3301C and EML 4140.

name: Vibrations
description: Single and multiple degree of freedom systems, including application to mechanical systems with problems employing computer techniques.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3344 and EGM 3401 and EGM 3520 and MAP 2302 with minimum grades of C.

name: Control of Dynamic Systems
description: Theory, analysis and design of control systems, including mechanical, electromechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal, and aerospace components and systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3401 and EGM 3344 and MAP 2302 with minimum grades of C.

name: Dynamics and Controls System Design Laboratory
description: Experiments on dynamic systems in mechanical and aerospace engineering and design of relevant control systems.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 3301C and EML 4312.

name: Manufacturing Engineering
description: Traditional and nontraditional manufacturing processes and equipment. Application of engineering analysis tools to manufacturing.
prerequisites: Prereq: EMA 3010 and EML 2322L and EML 3005.

name: Energy Conversion
description: Thermomechanical and thermoelectric energy conversion, conventional and unconventional techniques, and analysis of energy systems interactions.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 3100 with a minimum grade of C and EGN 3353C and EML 4140.

name: Mechanical Engineering Design 2
topics: roark's formulas for stress and strain
description: Integrated design and presentation of a mechanical system.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 4140 & EGN 3353C & EML 2322L & EML 3005 & (EGM 3401 with a minimum grade of C).

name: Mechanical Engineering Design 3
topics: roark’s formulas for stress and strain
description: Design and realization of a mechanical engineering system, component, or process subject to appropriate standards and constraints. Team project.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 4501 or EAS 4700 or EAS 4710; Coreq: EML 4321.

name: Finite Element Analysis and Design
description: Stress-strain analysis and design of machine elements and finite element analysis.
prerequisites: Prereq: EGM 3344 and EGM 3520 and MAP 2302 with minimum grades of C.

name: Heating and Air Conditioning System Design
topics: heating and air conditioning system design
description: Heating and air conditioning systems: equipment selection, system arrangement, load calculations, advanced psychrometrics, duct and piping system design, air distribution system design and indoor air quality.
prerequisites: Prereq: EML 3100.

name: Special Topics in Mechanical Engineering: Sensor Based Robot Planning
description: Variable content in mechanical engineering not offered in other courses.
prerequisites: Prereq: Engineering major of junior standing or higher.

name: Data Anal. for Indus. Apps.
topics: applied statistics and probability for engineers
statistical intervals for a single sample
description: Focuses on analysis of data encountered in ISE applications including system reliability, demand forecasting and inventory control, simulation, and quality control. Specific engineering applications are discussed through case studies. Introduction and use of computational tools to implement various data analysis techniques is an important component of this course.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2312 with minimum grade of C.

name: Operations Research 1
description: Introduces deterministic optimization modeling, algorithms, and software to aid in the analysis and solution of decision-making problems.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3327C with minimum grade of C.

name: Matrix and Numerical Methods in Systems Engineering
topics: ieee single precision floating point systems
ieee double precision floating point systems
description: Theory and application of vector, matrix and other numerical methods to systems problems. Simultaneous linear equations, characteristic values, quadratic forms, error analysis, use of series, curve fitting, nonlinear equations, discrete methods. The laboratory sessions will emphasize numerical solutions using common programming languages.
prerequisites: Prereq: MAC 2313 and MAS 3114 with minimum grades of C.

name: Operations Research 2
description: Introduces stochastic models and methodologies for analyzing and providing solutions to decision-making problems with uncertainties.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3327C and ESI 3215C with minimum grades of C.

name: Decision Support Systems for Industrial and Systems Engineers
description: Applications of decision support systems in industrial and systems engineering; developing and implementing decision support systems arising in industrial and systems engineering using popular database management and spreadsheet software.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP2273 or COP2271 with a minimum grade of C. Coreq: ESI 3312 will minimum grades of C.

name: Industrial Systems Simulation
description: Simulation methodology and languages, such as General Purpose Simulation System (GPSS). Design and analysis of simulation experiments as well as applications to solutions of industrial and service system problems.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP2273 or COP2271 and ESI 3215C with minimum grades of C.

name: Introduction to Data Analytics
description: Provides a basic understanding of the skills necessary for managing and analyzing data. The concepts covered include exploratory data analysis, data manipulation, data cleaning, data wrangling, and machine learning models. A basic understanding of data management with SQL is also provided. All the technical skills will be motivated by different examples involving data. Python is the programming language used.
prerequisites: Prereq: COP2273 or COP2271 and ESI 3215C with minimum grades of C.

name: Advanced Data Analytics
description: Second course in the data analytics ISE sequence that focuses on how and why algorithms work using an application-oriented approach. Studies advanced analytical and learning models that enhance decision making by converting data to information. Provides insights into how to choose the most effective tool for implementing a specific model.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 4610 with a minimum grade of C.

name: Decision Analytics Design
description: Studies methods for designing and evaluating analytics systems for optimizing decision making. Teaches technical and programming skills for implementation and feedback of an analytics pipeline from input data curation, processing, and validation, to prescribing outcomes.
prerequisites: Prereq: ESI 3312 with a minimum grade of C and (ESI 4610 or COP 2273 both with a minimum grade of C).

name: Transportation Engineering
description: Overview of the significance of highway transportation to the social and economic underpinnings of society. Introduces road vehicle performance, geometric design of highways, traffic flow and queuing theory, highway capacity and level of service analysis, traffic control and analysis at signalized intersections, and travel demand and traffic forecasting.
prerequisites: Prereq: 3EG classification.
